headline: Operational efficiency surged across the quarter as agent orchestration matured and Claude Fable 5 drew scrutiny over its safety filters.
body: Operational efficiency was the quarter's strongest mover, climbing to momentum 145 while agent, prompt caching, token optimization, and cache hit rate lagged, signaling a rotation from raw capability toward cost and throughput. Coding-agent scale was a recurring theme, with Cursor describing orchestration of 128 agents at once through inter-agent communication and review workflows, and tutorials reframing Claude Code as a systematic platform spanning subagents, hooks, and routines. Claude Fable 5 dominated the new items, both as a launch and as a backlash: users reported overly aggressive safety filters refusing harmless prompts and questioned whether they were served a downgraded fallback model. Newer entrants including ASIC accelerators, text-to-CAD, and faithful generation broke into the window alongside breakouts in attribution and collision detection.

cited_items.title: Master Claude Code with Claude Fable 5 (57 mins)
The Fable 5 Backlash Is Getting Serious
